title: Cooling Tower Approach Temperature
summary: The approach temperature is the difference between the leaving water temperature and the entering wet-bulb temperature.
evidence_quote: The approach temperature is the difference between the leaving water temperature and the entering wet-bulb temperature.

title: Cooling Tower Range
summary: The range is the temperature difference between the entering and leaving water.
evidence_quote: The range is the temperature difference between the entering and leaving water.

title: Cooling Tower Drift Loss
summary: Drift loss is typically 0.005 to 0.2% of the circulating water flow rate.
evidence_quote: Drift loss is typically 0.005 to 0.2% of the circulating water flow rate.

title: Cooling Tower Cycles of Concentration
summary: Cycles of concentration is the ratio of dissolved solids in the circulating water to that in the makeup water.
evidence_quote: Cycles of concentration is the ratio of dissolved solids in the circulating water to that in the makeup water.

title: Cooling Tower Water Treatment
summary: Water treatment is required to control scale, corrosion, and biological growth.
evidence_quote: Water treatment is required to control scale, corrosion, and biological growth.

title: Cooling Tower Fan Control
summary: Fans may be cycled on and off or modulated to maintain the desired leaving water temperature.
evidence_quote: Fans may be cycled on and off or modulated to maintain the desired leaving water temperature.

title: Cooling Tower Location
summary: Cooling towers should be located to avoid recirculation of discharge air and to minimize noise impact.
evidence_quote: Cooling towers should be located to avoid recirculation of discharge air and to minimize noise impact.

title: Cooling Tower Design Wet-Bulb Temperature
summary: The design wet-bulb temperature is typically selected based on the 1% annual cumulative frequency of occurrence.
evidence_quote: The design wet-bulb temperature is typically selected based on the 1% annual cumulative frequency of occurrence.

title: Cooling Tower Capacity Rating
summary: Cooling tower capacity is rated in tons, where 1 ton equals 15,000 Btu/h heat rejection.
evidence_quote: Cooling tower capacity is rated in tons, where 1 ton equals 15,000 Btu/h heat rejection.

title: Cooling Tower Seasonal Shutdown
summary: At seasonal shutdown, the tower should be drained and cleaned to prevent freezing and biological growth.
evidence_quote: At seasonal shutdown, the tower should be drained and cleaned to prevent freezing and biological growth.

title: Cooling Tower Makeup Water Quality
summary: Makeup water quality should be analyzed to determine the required treatment and cycles of concentration.
evidence_quote: Makeup water quality should be analyzed to determine the required treatment and cycles of concentration.

title: Cooling Tower Freeze Protection
summary: In cold climates, cooling towers require freeze protection such as basin heaters or indoor sumps.
evidence_quote: In cold climates, cooling towers require freeze protection such as basin heaters or indoor sumps.

title: Cooling Tower Bypass Valve Operation
summary: A bypass valve may be used to maintain minimum flow through the condenser when the tower is not operating.
evidence_quote: A bypass valve may be used to maintain minimum flow through the condenser when the tower is not operating.

title: Cooling Tower Evaporation Loss
summary: Evaporation loss is approximately 1% of the circulating water flow for each 10°F of range.
evidence_quote: Evaporation loss is approximately 1% of the circulating water flow for each 10°F of range.

title: Cooling Tower Blowdown Rate
summary: Blowdown rate is controlled to maintain the desired cycles of concentration.
evidence_quote: Blowdown rate is controlled to maintain the desired cycles of concentration.
